title: Neogaerrhinum filipes
taxonomy: Neogaerrhinum filipes is a genus of plants in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is a small, annual plant native to North Africa. Neogaerrhinum filipes is a member of the family Scrophulariaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Scrophulariaceae is part of the larger clade of asterids, a group of flowering plants. Neogaerrhinum filipes has been classified as a distinct genus based on its unique characteristics and morphology.

intro: Neogaerrhinum filipes, also known as the short-stalked gerrhinum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neogaerrhinum filipes include gerbilin, which is a sesquiterpene lactone with potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. In traditional herbal medicine, Neogaerrhinum filipes is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
